Apply a material to the Stanford Bunny model and render the scene in Houdini. The scene at '/home/ga/HoudiniProjects/bunny_scene.hipnc' already has the bunny model imported with a Venice Sunset HDRI environment light. Your task is: (1) Create a new Principled Shader material in the /mat context (Material Palette or Network Editor), (2) Set its base color to gold (approximately R=0.83, G=0.68, B=0.21), (3) Set metallic to 0.9 and roughness to 0.2 for a shiny gold look, (4) Assign this material to the bunny geometry object, (5) Set up a Mantra render node at /out if not present, (6) Render a single frame to '/home/ga/HoudiniProjects/renders/bunny_render.png', and (7) Save the scene as '/home/ga/HoudiniProjects/bunny_material.hipnc'.

Import a 3D model into Houdini. In the Network Editor at the /obj level, create a new Geometry node. Inside that Geometry node, add a File SOP node and set its file path to '/home/ga/HoudiniProjects/data/bunny.obj' to load the Stanford Bunny model. After importing, verify the model is visible in the Scene View (viewport). Then save the scene as '/home/ga/HoudiniProjects/imported_bunny.hipnc' using File > Save As.

Scientific journals increasingly require colorblind-accessible figures. Convert the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image into an accessible Magenta/Green/Blue composite by mapping the original Red channel to Magenta while keeping the Green and Blue channels unchanged. Save the final flattened RGB image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/accessible_composite.tif.

Scientific journals require microscopy figures to be accessible to color-blind readers (avoiding Red/Green combinations) and to include visible scale bars. Transform the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image into a publication-quality figure by changing the Red channel to Magenta while keeping the Green channel Green. Set the spatial calibration to 0.16 µm/pixel and include a 10 µm white scale bar in the lower right corner. Save the final flattened RGB image as a PNG to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/accessible_figure.png.

A microscopy image has been corrupted by both high-frequency noise and low-frequency uneven illumination. Clean the image located at ~/ImageJ_Data/raw/noisy_blobs.tif using an FFT Bandpass Filter. Filter out large structures (approximately 40 pixels) and small structures (approximately 3-5 pixels) without suppressing stripes. Save the filtered image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/filtered_image.tif. Create a CSV report at ~/ImageJ_Data/results/bandpass_filter_results.csv comparing the 'Mean' and 'StdDev' statistics of the image before and after filtering, including columns for 'Condition' (Before/After), 'Mean', and 'StdDev'.

Perform an object-based colocalization analysis on the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image to isolate regions where both red and green signals are present. Create binary masks for the red and green channels using automatic thresholding. Save the red mask to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/mask_red.tif and the green mask to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/mask_green.tif. Generate an intersection mask showing pixels where both masks overlap and save it to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/mask_overlap.tif. Count the discrete objects in the intersection mask and save the results to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/overlap_counts.csv.

Create a publication-quality figure panel from the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image. Split the image into its Red, Green, and Blue channels and create a montage containing all three individual channels plus the original composite image (4 panels total). Save the final montage as a TIFF file to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/fluorescence_montage.tif.

Perform advanced local contrast enhancement on the 'Embryos (42K)' microscopy sample image. Apply Contrast-Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) to the image to reveal local details. Create a side-by-side comparison montage showing the original image on the left and the enhanced version on the right. Save the final montage as a PNG file to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/clahe_comparison.png'.

You are designing a computer vision pipeline for vegetable sorting. Using the 'Peppers' sample image, create a binary mask that isolates only the green pepper, excluding the red and yellow peppers and the background. The resulting mask must use pixel values of 0 for the background and 255 for the green pepper. Save this binary mask as a PNG file to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/green_pepper_mask.png.

Perform particle analysis on the 'blobs' sample image in Fiji. Threshold the image to binary and ensure particles are represented as white objects on a black background. Run the particle analysis using a size filter of 10-Infinity pixels and a circularity range of 0.00-1.00. Save the measurement results to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/Results.csv and the summary statistics to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/Summary.csv. Report the total particle count and the average particle area (Average Size) from the summary data.

You are a cell biologist analyzing protein expression levels. Using the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image in Fiji, quantify the Corrected Total Cell Fluorescence (CTCF) for at least 5 individual cells in the green channel (tubulin). Calculate CTCF using the formula: CTCF = Integrated Density - (Area of Cell * Mean Background Fluorescence). Save the results to a CSV file at ~/ImageJ_Data/results/ctcf_results.csv including columns for Cell ID, Area, Integrated Density, Mean Background, and the calculated CTCF. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

Create a depth-coded (or Z-coded) color projection of the 'Fly Brain' sample stack where color represents the Z-depth of structures. Use the 'Fire' LUT (or a similar spectrum LUT) to project the full stack range. Save the resulting RGB color image as 'depth_coded_fly_brain.png' to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/'.

Perform Fourier-domain analysis on a transmission electron microscopy (TEM) image to determine pore periodicity. Open the built-in 'TEM Filter Plug' sample image in Fiji (File > Open Samples > TEM Filter Plug). Compute the 2D Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) to generate a power spectrum. Measure the distance (in pixels) from the center of the FFT spectrum to the dominant spatial frequency peaks. Calculate the real-space pore spacing using the formula: Spacing = Image_Width / Peak_Distance_From_Center. Save the FFT power spectrum image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/fft_power_spectrum.tif (or .png) and save a report to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/fft_periodicity_report.csv containing the measured 'Dominant_Period' in pixels and the 'Image_Width' used for calculation.

Perform pseudo-flat-field correction on the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image to fix uneven illumination. Document the mean intensity in four quadrants (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, and bottom-right) before and after correction to demonstrate improved uniformity. Save the corrected image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/flatfield_corrected.tif and a CSV report with columns 'Quadrant', 'Mean_Before', and 'Mean_After' to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/illumination_report.csv.

You are a cell biologist analyzing protein co-localization in fluorescence microscopy data. Using the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image in Fiji, perform a quantitative colocalization analysis between the red and green channels. Measure the thresholded area for each channel, the mean fluorescence intensity within each channel's thresholded region, and the pixel-level overlap area. Compute at least one colocalization coefficient (Pearson correlation coefficient r, Manders overlap coefficients M1/M2, or intersection-over-union). Save all measurements with clear column headers to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/colocalization_results.csv. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

You are a microscopy core facility technician evaluating the focus quality of a batch of 8 TIFF microscopy images located at ~/ImageJ_Data/focus_assessment/. Measure the Mean, Standard Deviation (StdDev), Min, and Max pixel intensities for at least 6 of the images. Record these statistics in a CSV file at ~/ImageJ_Data/results/focus_quality_report.csv with columns for Filename (or Label), Mean, StdDev, Min, and Max. Sort the entries by StdDev in descending order, as the standard deviation of pixel intensities serves as a proxy for image sharpness (higher StdDev indicates sharper focus).

Perform fractal geometry analysis on a leaf venation network using Fiji (ImageJ).

1. Open the built-in 'Leaf (36K)' sample image (File > Open Samples > Leaf (36K)).
2. Isolate the venation network by converting the image to 8-bit grayscale and applying a Threshold (Image > Adjust > Threshold). Adjust settings so the veins are clearly defined (foreground) against the background.
3. Measure the complexity of the network using the Fractal Box Count tool (Analyze > Tools > Fractal Box Count).
4. Also measure the Area Fraction (percentage of foreground pixels) of your thresholded image (Analyze > Measure).
5. Save a summary CSV file to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/fractal_venation_results.csv' that includes:
   - The calculated Fractal Dimension (D)
   - The box counting data (box sizes and counts) generated by the tool
   - The Area Fraction of the network

Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

You are a biochemist quantifying protein expression levels from a gel electrophoresis experiment. Using the 'Gel' sample image in Fiji, perform a gel analysis to quantify all visible lanes and bands. For each detectable band, calculate the background-corrected integrated optical density and express it as a percentage of the strongest band in its lane. Save a results table containing lane number, band position (pixels from top), background-corrected integrated intensity, and relative intensity percentage to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/gel_quantification.csv. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

You are a materials scientist characterizing an Au-Pb-Sn solder alloy. Using the 'AuPbSn 40' sample in Fiji, select a representative slice and measure the total length of the inter-phase boundaries. Calculate the boundary density as the total boundary length divided by the total image area. Save a CSV file to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/grain_boundary_analysis.csv' containing the image dimensions (width, height), total image area in pixels^2, total boundary length in pixels, and boundary density in pixels^-1. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

An astronomical image of a galaxy (~/ImageJ_Data/raw/noisy_galaxy.tif) has been corrupted by 'hot pixels' (single pixels with value 65535). Clean this image while preserving the scientific data (stars and galaxy structure). Remove the single-pixel noise without blurring the stars or the galaxy details; note that a simple Gaussian Blur is not acceptable as it destroys data resolution. Save the cleaned 16-bit image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/clean_galaxy.tif.

You are a cell biologist analyzing a 5D confocal movie of a dividing cell. Process the 'Mitosis' sample image to produce a 2D time-lapse video of the chromosomes (Channel 1/Red) using Maximum Intensity Z-Projection across all time frames. Save the resulting time-lapse to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/chromosomes_timelapse.tif. Quantify the Mean Gray Value of the entire image for every time frame and save the measurements to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/intensity_trace.csv. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

Calculate an Area-based H-Score for the Red channel of the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image. The H-Score quantifies staining intensity using three tiers: Low Positive (1+) for intensity 20-99, Medium Positive (2+) for intensity 100-179, and High Positive (3+) for intensity 180-255. Pixels below 20 are considered background/noise. Use the formula: H-Score = (1 * %Low) + (2 * %Medium) + (3 * %High), where %Tier is the percentage of the total stained area (all pixels >= 20) occupied by that tier. Save a CSV report to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/h_score_report.csv' with columns: Tier, Area, Percentage, and Score_Contribution.

As a medical physicist QAing a radiation treatment plan, perform an optical density calibration on the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image to convert it into a radiation dose map. Calibrate the image intensity using a 'Straight Line' function with the following standards: Pixel Intensity 0 (Black) = 5.0 Gy, Pixel Intensity 255 (White) = 0.0 Gy, and Unit: 'Gy'. Generate measurement statistics for the calibrated image showing physical dose values. Save the calibrated image to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/calibrated_dose_map.tif' and the measurement results table to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/dose_report.csv'.

Perform a logical masking operation on the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image to isolate the blobs while preserving their original grayscale intensity values. The background must be completely black (pixel value 0). Save the resulting 8-bit TIFF image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/masked_blobs.tif.

Isolate the internal voids (holes) within the particles of the 'Blobs' sample image using binary image arithmetic. The final image should show the internal holes as white objects on a black background, with the main particle bodies removed. Save the resulting 'holes-only' image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/void_mask.tif.

Quantify the spatial distribution of intracellular vesicles relative to the cell boundary using the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image. Generate a Euclidean Distance Map from the cell body (Green channel) using a Gaussian Blur (sigma=2) and Otsu threshold. Identify vesicles in the Red channel and measure their mean distance from the cell boundary using the distance map. Export the results table, ensuring it includes the 'Mean' column representing the distance of each vesicle, to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/vesicle_distribution.csv.

You are a biophysicist studying cell division kinetics. Your goal is to generate a kymograph (space-time plot) from the 'Mitosis' 5D hyperstack sample to visualize chromosome movement over time. Process the Red channel (chromosomes) by performing a Max Intensity Z-projection across all time frames. Save this intermediate time-lapse stack to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/mitosis_projection.tif. From this projection, generate a kymograph by reslicing along a line drawn through the center of the dividing cell, crossing both sets of chromosomes along the axis of division. Save the final kymograph image to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/mitosis_kymograph.tif. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

Create a publication-quality indexed segmentation map of the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image. Identify particles with a size range of 10 to infinity, excluding those on the edges. Measure the Area, Circularity, and Feret's Diameter for each particle. Generate a flattened RGB map where every particle is outlined and numbered. Save the measurements to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/particle_data.csv and the labeled map to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/labeled_map.png.

Calculate the specific leaf area (SLA) proxy by measuring the total projected area of the 'Leaf' sample image. Calibrate the spatial scale using the known physical width of 10 cm for the image field of view. The measurement must represent the solid leaf blade, requiring internal holes to be filled before calculating the area. Save the measurement results in square centimeters (cm^2) to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/leaf_area.csv.

Perform a morphological skeleton analysis on the 'Leaf' sample image to analyze its vein network. Generate a skeleton of the veins and perform a branch analysis. Export the results, including '# Branches', '# Junctions', and 'Average Branch Length', to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/skeleton_analysis.csv.

Perform a quantitative Full Width at Half Maximum (FWHM) analysis on at least 5 cell nuclei from the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image, specifically using the blue (DAPI) channel. Record the Nucleus ID, Peak Intensity, Background Intensity, FWHM (in pixels), and Line Length (in pixels) for each nucleus. Save these measurements to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/fwhm_measurements.csv. The CSV must include a summary row at the bottom with the Mean and Standard Deviation of the FWHM values.

Perform a local thickness analysis on the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image to characterize structural width. Create a binary mask of the objects and compute a 32-bit local thickness map where pixel values represent thickness. Save the thickness map to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/thickness_map.tif. Analyze the thickness distribution and save a CSV file containing the summary statistics (Mean, StdDev, Min, Max) to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/thickness_distribution.csv.

Automate a standard image analysis protocol by creating a reusable Fiji macro for the 'Blobs' sample image. The macro must perform a Gaussian Blur with Sigma 2.0, apply Otsu thresholding, and run Analyze Particles with a size range of 20-Infinity showing outlines. Save the resulting script as 'standard_protocol.ijm' to '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/macros/'.

Perform manual tracking of a dividing cell in the 'Mitosis' sample image. Create a Max Intensity Z-Projection to produce a 2D time-lapse. Identify the cell near (X=145, Y=130) at Frame 1 and record its X, Y centroid coordinates (in pixels) for Frames 1, 6, 11, 16, and 21. For Frame 21, record the right-most daughter cell. Save the results in CSV format (Frame, X, Y) to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/tracking_trace.csv.

You are a researcher preparing a ground-truth dataset for a cell classification model. Using Fiji (ImageJ), manually classify objects in the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image into two categories: 'Round' and 'Elongated'. Mark at least 5 'Round' objects and at least 5 'Elongated' objects using distinct counters. Export the coordinates (X, Y) and counter/type information for all marked points to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/manual_counts.csv'.

You have access to Fiji (ImageJ) with the BBBC005 synthetic cell dataset from the Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection. This dataset contains simulated fluorescent images of cells with known ground truth, making it ideal for validating cell measurement algorithms. Your task is to analyze a cell image from ~/ImageJ_Data/raw/BBBC005/ (such as SIMCEPImages_A02_C19_F1_s06_w2.TIF) to segment and measure the synthetic fluorescent cells. Process the image using Gaussian blur (sigma=2), thresholding, and watershed segmentation to separate touching cells. Perform particle analysis with a size range of 100-5000 pixels and circularity of 0.3-1.0. Report the total number of cells detected, the average cell area in pixels, and the average circularity.

Process the 'Mitosis' sample dataset into a presentation-ready video format. Perform a Maximum Intensity Z-Projection on the 5D hyperstack and isolate the Red channel (Channel 1, Chromatin). Create a sub-stack containing only the first 20 timepoints and add a timestamp starting at 00:00 with a 15.00-second interval in the top-left corner (font size 20-25). Save the final TIFF stack to ~/ImageJ_Data/processed/mitosis_movie_formatted.tif.

You are a cell biologist analyzing cell division dynamics from a time-lapse fluorescence experiment. Using the 'Mitosis (5D stack)' sample in Fiji, analyze the multi-dimensional hyperstack to track mitotic events across all available time frames. For each time point, generate a maximum-intensity Z-projection from the channel that best highlights mitotic events. Perform thresholding to identify mitotic cells or nuclei, then calculate the object count and total fluorescent area for each projected time frame. Save a CSV containing the time frame index, object count, and total thresholded area to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/mitosis_timeseries.csv. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

You are a bioimage analyst preparing a dataset for a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). Open the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image and transform it into a standardized tensor format. Invert the image contrast so objects appear bright on a dark background. Resize the image to 128 pixels wide while maintaining the aspect ratio, then pad the canvas to exactly 256x256 pixels (centered with a black background). Save the resulting TIFF file to ~/ImageJ_Data/processed/blobs_ml_ready.tif. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

Perform a systematic analysis of how binary morphological operations affect particle segmentation metrics using the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image. For the baseline binary image and four morphological variations (Erode, Dilate, Open, and Close), record the Operation Name, Particle Count, Total Area, and Average Particle Size. Save the compiled results with headers to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/morphological_series.csv.

Perform a quantitative image quality assessment on the 'T1 Head (2.4M)' sample MRI brain scan. Measure the Mean Intensity and Standard Deviation for White Matter (WM), Gray Matter (GM), and Background (BG) regions on a mid-brain axial slice. Calculate SNR_WM (Mean_WM / Std_BG), SNR_GM (Mean_GM / Std_BG), and CNR ((Mean_WM - Mean_GM) / Std_BG). Save the raw measurements and computed metrics with clear labels to a CSV file at ~/ImageJ_Data/results/mri_snr_cnr.csv.

You need to create a lightweight 'web preview' version of a large MRI scan for email sharing. Open the 'T1 Head' sample image and process it by converting it to 8-bit grayscale, scaling the spatial dimensions to 75% of the original size, and subsampling the stack to keep only every 3rd slice. Save the resulting processed stack as a TIFF file to ~/ImageJ_Data/processed/mri_preview.tif.

Estimate the total brain tissue volume of the "T1 Head" MRI sample stack. Segment the brain tissue from the background, CSF, and skull to measure the tissue area across the slices. Save the per-slice area measurements and the total volume estimate to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/mri_volume_results.csv. The CSV must include a slice identifier column, the measured area per slice, and the total volume value. Note that tissue area varies across the 129 slices of this T1-weighted scan.

Perform quantitative time-series analysis on the 'Mitosis (26MB, 5D stack)' sample image. Create a Maximum Intensity Projection to collapse the Z-dimension while preserving the time series, then analyze the Red channel (Microtubules). Identify at least 3 distinct cells or regions and generate intensity traces for these regions across all 51 time frames. Save the measurement results to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/time_series_traces.csv' and the ROI set to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/cell_rois.zip'.

You are a microscopist processing raw data from an experiment. Create a calibrated, multi-channel composite image using the files in ~/ImageJ_Data/raw/experiment_042/. Assign mitochondria_raw.tif to Channel 1 (Red), cytoskeleton_raw.tif to Channel 2 (Green), and nucleus_raw.tif to Channel 3 (Blue). Apply spatial calibration in microns based on the pixel size information provided in microscope_metadata.txt. Ensure the cytoskeleton in the Green channel is clearly visible through brightness and contrast adjustments. Save the resulting multi-channel composite TIFF to ~/ImageJ_Data/processed/assembled_cell.tif.

Perform a multi-channel intensity analysis on the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image to measure the signal intensity of the Red, Green, and Blue channels within three distinct nuclei. Record the 'Area' and 'Mean Gray Value' for each nucleus across all three channels and save the resulting table as 'nuclei_stack_profiles.csv' to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/'.

Measure the nuclear-to-cytoplasmic (N/C) fluorescence intensity ratio for at least 3 individual cells in the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image. Use the Blue channel to define nuclear regions and the Green channel to measure mean intensities for both nuclear and cytoplasmic regions. Calculate the N/C ratio (Mean Nuclear Intensity / Mean Cytoplasmic Intensity) for each cell and save a results table containing the Nuclear Mean, Cytoplasmic Mean, and N/C Ratio for each cell to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/nc_ratio_results.csv.

You are a microbiologist analyzing a culture containing two bacterial strains of different sizes. Using the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image, segment the particles and classify them into two distinct populations based on area. Filter out noise smaller than 60 pixels. Classify the remaining particles into 'Small Population' (area between 60 and 350 pixels inclusive) and 'Large Population' (area greater than 350 pixels). Save the statistical measurements including the 'Area' column for each group to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/small_particles.csv and ~/ImageJ_Data/results/large_particles.csv. Create a classification map image where 'Small' particles are colored BLUE and 'Large' particles are colored GREEN, and save it to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/classification_map.png.

Perform a particle size distribution analysis on the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image. Segment the particles, filtering out noise smaller than 10 px^2 and excluding edge particles. Calculate the Area, Perimeter, and Equivalent Circular Diameter (d = 2 * sqrt(Area / pi)) for each particle. Generate a CSV report at ~/ImageJ_Data/results/size_distribution.csv containing the individual particle data, summary statistics (Count, Mean, Median, Standard Deviation, Coefficient of Variation, Min, and Max of the diameters), and size bin distributions for Small (d < 20), Medium (20 <= d <= 35), and Large (d > 35).

Perform a shape morphometry analysis on the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image to identify the 'roughest' particle, defined as the one with the lowest Solidity value. Include 'Solidity', 'Area', and 'Display Label' in the measurements and filter out particles with an area less than 50 pixels. Save the complete results table to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/solidity_measurements.csv'. Record the Label/ID and Solidity value of the roughest particle in '~/ImageJ_Data/results/roughest_particle.txt'.

You are a metallurgical engineer analyzing a gold-lead-tin alloy. Your task is to quantify the volume fraction of the three distinct phases in the microstructure using the 'AuPbSn 40' sample image. The phases are distinguished by brightness: the Gold-rich phase (brightest), the Intermetallic phase (medium), and the Matrix phase (darkest). Segment the image based on grayscale intensity thresholds to measure the area and calculate the area fraction (%) for each phase. Save the results to a CSV file at ~/ImageJ_Data/results/phase_fractions.csv containing columns for Phase Name, Threshold Range, Area (pixels), and Area Fraction (%). Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

Perform a ratiometric analysis on the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image. Generate a 32-bit floating-point ratio image (Red Channel / Green Channel) restricted to the cell area defined by the Green channel mask, with background pixels set to 0 or NaN. Save the resulting ratio image to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/ratio_map.tif'. Record the mean intensity of the ratio image within the cell mask to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/mean_ratio.txt'.

You are an environmental hydrologist analyzing the Nile River. Extract morphometric parameters from the 'Nile Bend' sample image. Measure the total water surface area in pixels squared, the river channel width at three distinct cross-sections (upstream, bend apex, and downstream), and the channel sinuosity (calculated as channel length divided by straight-line distance). Save these measurements, including the area, the three widths, and the sinuosity index, to a CSV file at ~/ImageJ_Data/results/river_morphometry.csv.

Perform a quantitative signal-to-background ratio (SBR) analysis on the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image in Fiji. Define and name at least two ROIs for bright cell structures (including 'signal' or 'cell' in the name), at least one ROI for a dim cytoplasmic region (including 'cytoplasm' or 'dim'), and at least one ROI for the dark background (including 'background' or 'bg'). Measure the Mean, Standard Deviation, and Area for these regions. Calculate the SBR using the formula: (Average Mean of Signal ROIs) / (Mean of Background ROI). Save the ROI set to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/roi_set.zip and the measurements table to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/roi_measurements.csv. Save the JASP analysis file to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/LogisticRegression_Cloak.jasp. Additionally, create a summary text file at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/logistic_report.txt containing the following labeled values from the results: the coefficient (B), odds ratio (OR), and p-value for 'Mischief', the classification accuracy percentage, and the Nagelkerke R² value.

Perform a Mann-Whitney U test on the 'Invisibility Cloak' dataset (/home/ga/Documents/JASP/InvisibilityCloak.csv) to analyze mischief behavior. Compare 'Mischief' as the dependent variable between the 'Cloak' and 'No Cloak' grouping conditions. Include rank biserial correlation effect size and descriptive statistics in the analysis. Save the JASP project to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/MannWhitneyCloak.jasp and create a text report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/mann_whitney_report.txt containing the test statistic (W or U), p-value, rank biserial correlation, sample sizes, and a one-sentence conclusion regarding significance at alpha=0.05.

The file '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety.csv' is already open in JASP. This dataset (Field, 2013) contains data from 103 students on Exam performance (%), Anxiety level, Revision time, and Gender. Perform a One-Way MANOVA to test for gender differences in both Exam performance and Anxiety simultaneously. Include descriptive statistics in the analysis and save the JASP file as '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety_MANOVA.jasp'.

Perform a mediation analysis to test if revision time ('Revise') mediates the relationship between exam anxiety ('Anxiety') and exam performance ('Exam'). Save the JASP project to '~/Documents/JASP/MediationAnalysis.jasp'. Create a text report at '~/Documents/JASP/mediation_report.txt' including the coefficients for Path a (Anxiety -> Revise), Path b (Revise -> Exam), the Indirect Effect estimate, the Direct Effect estimate, and a conclusion stating whether the results represent 'Full Mediation', 'Partial Mediation', or 'No Mediation'.

Perform a moderation analysis using the dataset at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety.csv to test if Exam Anxiety moderates the effect of Revision time on Exam performance. Include an interaction term between Revise and Anxiety in the linear regression model and generate a simple slopes analysis with Revise on the x-axis and Anxiety as the moderator. Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Moderation_Analysis.jasp.

Using the dataset at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Viagra.csv, which contains data from a pharmacological study on the effect of Viagra on libido, perform a Multinomial Goodness-of-Fit Test on the 'Dose' variable to check if participants were distributed according to the study protocol. Test the hypothesis of a 50/25/25 split (Placebo: 0.5, Low Dose: 0.25, High Dose: 0.25). Include descriptives and a descriptives plot in the analysis. Save the JASP project to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/MultinomialGOF.jasp.

Perform a Naive Bayes multiclass classification on the ToothGrowth dataset to predict vitamin C dose levels using 'len' and 'supp' as predictors. Ensure the 'dose' variable is treated as a categorical (nominal) variable. Use either a 70/30 training/test split or 5-fold cross-validation with a seed of 42. The analysis should include the confusion matrix and classification accuracy. Save the JASP analysis file to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/NaiveBayesDose.jasp and create a text report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/naive_bayes_report.txt including the algorithm name, the overall classification accuracy, the confusion matrix counts, and a sentence interpreting which dose level was easiest or hardest to classify.

Perform a network analysis on the Big Five Personality Traits dataset (/home/ga/Documents/JASP/BigFivePersonalityTraits.csv) to understand the connections between personality dimensions. Analyze the variables Neuroticism, Extraversion, Openness, Agreeableness, and Conscientiousness using the EBICglasso estimator. Ensure the analysis includes a Centrality Plot and Weights Matrix. Save the JASP project to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/BigFiveNetwork.jasp. Create a text report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/network_report.txt identifying the variable with the highest Strength centrality, the variable with the highest Betweenness centrality, the pair of variables with the strongest edge weight (positive or negative), and the total number of non-zero edges in the network.

Train a Neural Network regression model to predict abalone age using the dataset from https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/abalone/abalone.data. Save the data to ~/Documents/JASP/abalone.csv with the following headers: 'Sex', 'Length', 'Diameter', 'Height', 'Whole_weight', 'Shucked_weight', 'Viscera_weight', 'Shell_weight', 'Rings'. Configure the regression model with 'Rings' as the target and all other variables as predictors, using an 80/20 training/test split and a seed of 123. The neural network architecture must consist of two hidden layers with 8 nodes in the first layer and 4 nodes in the second. Save the JASP project to ~/Documents/JASP/Abalone_NeuralNet.jasp and record the Test MSE value in ~/Documents/JASP/abalone_performance.txt.

A heart rate study dataset is loaded in JASP (file: '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/HeartRate.csv'). This dataset contains 800 observations from a study comparing resting heart rates between runners and sedentary controls, with participants of both genders. The three columns are: 'Gender' (Female/Male), 'Group' (Control/Runners), and 'Heart Rate' (beats per minute). The researchers suspect that the heart rate data may not meet parametric assumptions and want nonparametric analyses. Perform a Kruskal-Wallis test with 'Heart Rate' as the dependent variable and 'Group' as the grouping variable, including descriptive statistics. Conduct a Mann-Whitney U test comparing 'Heart Rate' between 'Gender' groups, including descriptive statistics and the effect size. Generate descriptive statistics for 'Heart Rate' split by 'Group', including boxplots and distribution plots. Save the completed analysis file as '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/heart_rate_nonparametric.jasp'.

The dataset '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/Viagra.csv' is already open and contains data from a pharmacological study (Field, 2013) examining the effect of Viagra on libido. Perform a One-Way ANOVA to analyze the effect of 'dose' on 'libido'. The analysis must include descriptive statistics and the η² (eta-squared) effect size.

Perform an Ordinal Logistic Regression analysis using the Big Five Personality Traits dataset (/home/ga/Documents/JASP/BigFivePersonalityTraits.csv) to predict the response to item 'A1' ('Am indifferent to the feelings of others') based on 'Gender' and 'Age'. Ensure 'A1' is treated as an ordinal variable and include the McFadden R² value and the Test of parallel lines (Proportional odds assumption) in the analysis. Save the JASP project to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Ordinal_A1_Analysis.jasp and create a text report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ordinal_report.txt containing the McFadden R² value, the p-value for the Test of parallel lines, and a statement on whether the proportional odds assumption holds.

Perform a multivariate outlier analysis on the Exam Anxiety dataset (/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety.csv). Conduct a linear regression using 'Exam' as the dependent variable and 'Anxiety' and 'Revise' as covariates to determine the Mahalanobis distance for each case. Identify the student 'Code' associated with the highest Mahalanobis distance and record only that ID in /home/ga/Documents/JASP/outlier_report.txt. Save the completed JASP project to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/OutlierAnalysis.jasp.

Using the weight gain study dataset at '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/WeightGain.csv', perform a Paired Samples T-Test comparing 'Weight Before' and 'Weight After' using Student's t-test, including Cohen's d effect size and descriptive statistics. Additionally, generate descriptive statistics (mean, standard deviation, minimum, maximum, and median) for 'Weight Before', 'Weight After', and 'Difference'. Save the complete analysis to '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/weight_gain_analysis.jasp'.

Using the sleep dataset at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/SleepWide.csv, conduct a Paired Samples T-Test comparing 'Drug1' and 'Drug2'. The analysis must include Cohen's d effect size, descriptive statistics, and a raincloud difference plot. Save the completed JASP analysis file to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/SleepPairedTTest.jasp.

Determine the partial correlation between 'Exam' and 'Anxiety' in the Exam Anxiety dataset (/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety.csv), controlling for 'Revise'. The analysis should include significance reporting, 95% confidence intervals, and scatter plots. Save the analysis to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/PartialCorrelation_Exam.jasp.

Perform a Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on the Big Five Personality Traits dataset located at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/BigFivePersonalityTraits.csv. Include all five personality variables (Agreeableness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Neuroticism, and Openness) in the analysis. Configure the PCA using varimax rotation and extraction based on Eigenvalues greater than 1. The final output must include a scree plot and a path diagram. Save the analysis as a JASP file to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/BigFive_PCA.jasp.

Test the 'Yerkes-Dodson law' (inverted U-shape relationship between anxiety and performance) using the loaded Exam Anxiety dataset. Perform a polynomial regression analysis with 'Exam' as the dependent variable, using 'Anxiety' and a computed squared 'Anxiety' variable as predictors. Include descriptive statistics in the output and save the analysis to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Polynomial_Exam.jasp.

Perform a replication power analysis in JASP using the dataset '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/InvisibilityCloak.csv'. Determine the observed Cohen's d from an Independent Samples T-Test (Mischief by Cloak). Calculate the total sample size required for a two-sided replication with a power of 0.90 and an alpha of 0.05 based on the observed effect size. Save the JASP project to '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/PowerAnalysis.jasp' and record both the observed Cohen's d and the calculated Total Sample Size in '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/power_report.txt'.

Perform a Process Capability Analysis on the 'len' variable from the Tooth Growth dataset. 

1. Open '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ToothGrowth.csv'.
2. Activate the 'Quality Control' module (click the '+' icon in the top right if not visible).
3. Select 'Process Capability' analysis.
4. Assign 'len' to Variables.
5. Set Lower specification limit (LSL) to 5.
6. Set Upper specification limit (USL) to 35.
7. Save the JASP project as '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/capability_analysis.jasp'.
8. Create a text file '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/cpk_report.txt' and write the calculated Cpk value (e.g., 0.123) into it.

Perform a Quality Control analysis on the 'E1' item from the Big Five Personality Traits dataset (/home/ga/Documents/JASP/BigFivePersonalityTraits.csv) already open in JASP. Generate an X-bar & R control chart using 'E1' as the measurement variable with a subgroup size of 10 defined by observation number. Determine if the process is 'In Control' or 'Out of Control' based on whether any data points fall outside the control limits on the X-bar chart. Save the JASP project to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/qc_analysis.jasp and create a report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/qc_report.txt containing the status in the format 'Status: In Control' or 'Status: Out of Control'.

Generate a grouped descriptive analysis of the Tooth Growth dataset located at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ToothGrowth.csv. Configure the dose variable as an ordinal type. Perform a descriptive statistics analysis on len split by both supp and dose, including the Mean, Standard Deviation, and 95% Confidence Interval for the Mean. Create a raincloud plot visualization using a colorblind-friendly palette. Save the completed analysis as /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ToothGrowth_Raincloud.jasp.

Perform a multiple linear regression analysis in JASP using the dataset at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety.csv to predict 'Exam' scores based on 'Anxiety' and 'Revise'. The analysis must include collinearity diagnostics (VIF) and part and partial correlations. Save the JASP project as /home/ga/Documents/JASP/RegressionDiagnostics.jasp. Additionally, create a text report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/diagnostics_report.txt documenting the Anxiety VIF, Anxiety Part Correlation, and an interpretation of whether multicollinearity is a concern.

The file '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/WorldHappiness.csv' is already open in JASP. This is a real dataset from the World Happiness Report containing data for 155 countries. Your research question is: What national-level factors best predict a country's happiness score? Perform a linear regression analysis using 'Happiness Score' as the dependent variable and 'GDP per Capita', 'Family', 'Life Expectancy', and 'Freedom' as predictors. The analysis must include coefficient estimates, model fit measures (R-squared and adjusted R-squared), ANOVA, and collinearity diagnostics (VIF). Additionally, generate residual diagnostic plots, specifically a Q-Q plot of residuals and a residuals vs. predicted plot. Save the analysis to '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/happiness_regression.jasp'.

Perform a reliability analysis on the Agreeableness subscale (items A1, A2, A3, A4, A5) of the Big Five Personality Traits dataset found in '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/BigFivePersonalityTraits.csv'. Item A1 ('Am indifferent to the feelings of others') is reverse-coded and must be handled accordingly. The analysis should include McDonald's omega, Cronbach's alpha, Mean, and Standard deviation for the scale, as well as Cronbach's alpha (if item dropped), McDonald's omega (if item dropped), and Item-rest correlation for individual items. 

Save the JASP project to '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/Agreeableness_Reliability.jasp' and create a text report at '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/reliability_report.txt' using the format below, rounding metrics to 3 decimal places and Mean/SD to 2 decimal places:

Scale: Agreeableness (A1-A5)
Number of items: 5
Reverse-coded items: A1
Cronbach's Alpha: <value>
McDonald's Omega: <value>
Scale Mean: <value>
Scale SD: <value>
Weakest Item: <item name>
Alpha If Weakest Dropped: <value>

Perform a Repeated Measures ANOVA on the 'Bugs.csv' dataset (Field, 2013) to analyze the desire to kill bugs based on Disgust (Low vs High) and Frequency (Low vs High). Map the columns LDLF (Low Disgust, Low Frequency), LDHF (Low Disgust, High Frequency), HDLF (High Disgust, Low Frequency), and HDHF (High Disgust, High Frequency) to the appropriate factor levels. The analysis must include descriptive statistics, partial η² effect sizes, and sphericity tests. Save the analysis file to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/BugsRM.jasp. Generate a summary report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/bugs_rm_report.txt containing the F-value, p-value, and partial η² for the Disgust, Frequency, and interaction effects, the Mean and SD for all four conditions, and the significance of Mauchly's test.

Perform a Random Forest Regression on the 'Exam Anxiety' dataset to predict 'Exam' scores using 'Anxiety' and 'Revise' as predictors. Configure the model with 100 trees and 80% training data, ensuring the analysis includes a predictive performance plot and variable importance. Save the JASP project to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/rf_exam_analysis.jasp. Create a text report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/rf_report.txt containing the Test Set MSE, Test Set R2, and the name of the most important predictor using the following format:
Line 1: MSE: <value>
Line 2: R2: <value>
Line 3: Most Important Predictor: <variable name>

Create an exploratory scatterplot using the 'ExamAnxiety.csv' dataset. The plot must feature 'Anxiety' on the X-axis and 'Exam' on the Y-axis, including a smooth (Loess) regression line and marginal histograms. Save the resulting analysis to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExploratoryPlot.jasp.

Perform a structural Path Analysis on the 'Exam Anxiety' dataset (/home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety.csv) to test the 'Anxiety-Interference' hypothesis. Specify a model where Revision time predicts Anxiety and Anxiety predicts Exam performance, ensuring no direct path exists between Revision and Exam. The analysis must include standardized estimates, R-squared values, and a path diagram. Save the analysis to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Exam_Path_Model.jasp. Additionally, create a text file at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Model_Fit_Report.txt containing the Chi-square test p-value and a one-sentence conclusion on whether this restricted model fits the data (using p > 0.05 as the threshold for good fit).

Perform a survival analysis on the lung cancer dataset located at ~/Documents/JASP/lung.csv. Conduct a Kaplan-Meier analysis using 'time' as the time variable, 'status' as the event status (with 2 indicating the event), and 'sex' as the strata. Save the completed analysis to ~/Documents/JASP/LungSurvival.jasp.

Train an SVM classifier in JASP using the 'BigFivePersonalityTraits.csv' dataset to predict 'Gender' from the five personality traits (Agreeableness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Neuroticism, and Openness). Use an 80/20 training/test data split with a seed of 12345 and an RBF kernel. Ensure the Confusion Matrix (Test set), ROC Curve, and Performance metrics (AUC) are enabled in the output. Save the JASP project to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/SVM_Gender_Analysis.jasp. Record the Test Set AUC value and the counts of True Positives and True Negatives from the Test Set Confusion Matrix in /home/ga/Documents/JASP/svm_performance_report.txt.

Perform a time series forecast on the airline passenger data located at ~/Documents/JASP/AirlinePassengers.csv. Using the Prophet forecasting module, set 'Passengers' as the dependent variable and 'Month' as the time variable. Configure the analysis to forecast 12 months and produce a plot. Save the final results to ~/Documents/JASP/PassengerForecast.jasp.

Perform a One-Sample Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test on the 'Exam' variable from the dataset at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/ExamAnxiety.csv to determine if the median significantly differs from a test value of 50. The analysis must include the Hodges-Lehmann estimate (location parameter) with its confidence interval and descriptive statistics. Save the analysis file to /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Exam_Wilcoxon_Test.jasp and create a text report at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/median_analysis_report.txt containing the Test Statistic (W), the p-value, and the Hodges-Lehmann estimate value.

Create an audit logging system for the Chinook database in LibreOffice Base. Execute the following SQL commands to create the audit table, the insert trigger, the delete trigger, and a test record. Save the database file to persist the changes.

CREATE TABLE PUBLIC."CustomerAuditLog" (
    "AuditId" INTEGER IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
    "CustomerId" INTEGER,
    "ActionType" VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    "ActionTimestamp" TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "Details" VARCHAR(200)
)

CREATE TRIGGER "trg_customer_insert" AFTER INSERT ON PUBLIC."Customer"
REFERENCING NEW ROW AS newrow
FOR EACH ROW
INSERT INTO PUBLIC."CustomerAuditLog" ("CustomerId","ActionType","Details")
VALUES (newrow."CustomerId", 'INSERT', newrow."FirstName" || ' ' || newrow."LastName")

CREATE TRIGGER "trg_customer_delete" AFTER DELETE ON PUBLIC."Customer"
REFERENCING OLD ROW AS oldrow
FOR EACH ROW
INSERT INTO PUBLIC."CustomerAuditLog" ("CustomerId","ActionType","Details")
VALUES (oldrow."CustomerId", 'DELETE', oldrow."FirstName" || ' ' || oldrow."LastName")

INSERT INTO PUBLIC."Customer" ("CustomerId","FirstName","LastName","Email")
VALUES (60, 'Audit', 'TestUser', 'audit.test@example.com')

Create two saved SQL Views in the Chinook database (`MonthlyRevenue` and `CumulativeRevenue`) to calculate monthly sales and running total revenue.

1. **Create View `MonthlyRevenue`**:
   - Source: `Invoice` table.
   - Columns: `SalesYear` (Year of InvoiceDate), `SalesMonth` (Month of InvoiceDate), `PeriodId` (Formula: Year * 100 + Month), and `MonthlyTotal` (Sum of Total).
   - Group by Year and Month.

2. **Create View `CumulativeRevenue`**:
   - Source: The `MonthlyRevenue` view you just created.
   - Columns: `SalesYear`, `SalesMonth`, `MonthlyTotal`, and `RunningTotal`.
   - `RunningTotal` must be the cumulative sum of `MonthlyTotal` for all periods up to and including the current one (ordered by `PeriodId`). Use a correlated subquery to calculate this.

3. **Save the database** (Ctrl+S) to persist your changes.

Create a text file at /sdcard/gout_safety_check.txt with the format:
Mercaptopurine + Allopurinol: [COLOR]
Mercaptopurine + Colchicine: [COLOR]
Recommendation: Use [SAFER_DRUG]

Line 2: Traffic light color of the SAFER drug
Line 3: Name of the RISKY drug
Line 4: Traffic light color of the RISKY drug

Compare the safety of two muscle relaxants, tizanidine and baclofen, for a patient taking the cancer drug rucaparib. Rucaparib + Tizanidine: [COLOR]
Rucaparib + Baclofen: [COLOR]

Implement a stored procedure named 'HumanResources.usp_RefreshWorkforceSummary' that repopulates the table by joining HumanResources.Department, HumanResources.EmployeeDepartmentHistory, HumanResources.Employee, and HumanResources.EmployeePayHistory. The procedure must identify current employees, calculate statistics using the most recent pay rate for each employee, and provide the gender breakdown. Execute the procedure to populate the table with the final workforce summary.

You are a cost engineer at a manufacturing company. Management needs lifecycle material cost reports for all assembled products in the AdventureWorks2022 database. Build a multi-level BOM cost reporting system that includes a recursive view 'dbo.vw_ProductBOMHierarchy' to fully traverse active rows (EndDate IS NULL) in Production.BillOfMaterials, exposing assembly and component identities, names, hierarchy depth, quantity, and unit of measure. The system must also include a cost summary table 'Production.LifecycleCostSummary' (with a non-clustered index on AssemblyProductID) that stores assembly identities, total component counts across all levels, maximum hierarchy depth, direct material costs (level 1 components only), full BOM material costs (all levels), and report timestamps. Implement and execute a stored procedure 'dbo.usp_GenerateBOMCostReport' that populates the summary table by querying the hierarchy view and computing the required cost metrics. The final implementation must demonstrate multi-level traversal and contain populated cost data for at least five assemblies.

You are a software developer working with the Chinook digital music store database (pre-loaded as 'chinook' in the MySQL server, host: localhost, user: ga, password: password123). Resolve data integrity issues by updating Invoice records with NULL BillingAddress using the Address from the corresponding Customer record, and updating InvoiceLine records where UnitPrice is inconsistent with the Track table's UnitPrice. Create a database view named v_sales_by_genre that joins Genre, Track, InvoiceLine, and Invoice to show total sales (SUM of UnitPrice * Quantity) per genre, with columns genre_name and total_sales. Add a non-unique index named idx_invoiceline_trackid on InvoiceLine(TrackId). Export the v_sales_by_genre results, sorted by total_sales descending, to /home/ga/Documents/exports/chinook_genre_sales.csv.

Connect to the local MySQL server (host: localhost, user: ga, password: password123) and query the Sakila database to find all films with a rental rate strictly greater than 2.99. The query must return the title and rental_rate columns from the sakila.film table. Export the results to /home/ga/Documents/exports/expensive_films.csv.

Implement a database audit and customer loyalty system for the Sakila DVD rental database (host: localhost, user: ga, password: password123). The supporting tables have already been created. Create an AFTER UPDATE trigger named tr_customer_audit on sakila.customer that inserts records into sakila.customer_audit_log containing the customer_id, old email, new email, and change timestamp. Develop a stored procedure named sp_calculate_loyalty_tiers() that clears and repopulates the sakila.customer_loyalty table, categorizing customers into 'Bronze' (0–5 rentals), 'Silver' (6–20 rentals), and 'Gold' (21+ rentals) tiers based on total rental counts. Verify the trigger by updating five customer email addresses, execute the loyalty procedure, and export the sakila.customer_loyalty table to /home/ga/Documents/exports/customer_loyalty.csv.

As a business intelligence analyst at a DVD rental company, you need to set up a complete analytics layer on the Sakila database (host: localhost, user: ga, password: password123). Create a view named v_film_revenue_by_store in the sakila schema that shows each film's rental count and total payment revenue broken down by store, including columns film_id, film_title, store_id, rental_count, and total_revenue. Create a view named v_customer_lifetime_value in the sakila schema that shows each customer's total payments and rental activity, including columns customer_id, customer_name, rental_count, total_spent, and avg_payment. Create a MySQL user 'reporter'@'localhost' with password 'Report2024!' and grant SELECT privilege on both views to this user. Export the full contents of v_customer_lifetime_value to /home/ga/Documents/exports/customer_lifetime_value.csv.

The Sakila DVD rental database is experiencing significant query performance degradation due to missing indexes on critical columns. Identify and restore missing indexes on the rental, payment, and inventory tables in the local MySQL server (host: localhost, user: ga, password: password123) to ensure efficient query execution. Create a database view named v_monthly_revenue in the sakila schema with columns payment_year, payment_month, and total_revenue that aggregates payment amounts by year and month. Create a stored procedure named sp_monthly_revenue(p_year INT) in the sakila schema that returns monthly revenue data for the given year. Export the results of calling sp_monthly_revenue(2005) to /home/ga/Documents/exports/monthly_revenue_2005.csv. Login: user=ga, password=password123.

The world database has been corrupted by a failed data import operation. Using MySQL Workbench (host: localhost, user: ga, password: password123), perform a data quality audit and cleanup of the city table. Remove referential integrity violations where city.CountryCode does not match any row in the country table, delete exact duplicate city records (matching Name, CountryCode, and District), and delete cities with a Population of 0. Create a channel named 'ADT Filter Channel' in NextGen Connect that listens on TCP port 6662 and filters HL7 messages to only accept ADT types (MSH-9), rejecting all other message types. Ensure the channel XML includes a <responseTransformer> element in destination connectors. Verify the filter logic using the test messages at /home/ga/sample_adt_message.hl7 and /home/ga/sample_oru_message.hl7. The REST API is available at https://localhost:8443/api (admin/admin) and requires the 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI' header. The web dashboard at https://localhost:8443 is monitoring-only. You can use the terminal window to interact with the system. Note: File Writer output files are created inside the Docker container.

Create and deploy a 'Patient Admission Channel' in NextGen Connect Integration Engine that accepts HL7 v2.x messages on TCP port 6661 using MLLP framing and writes received messages to /tmp/hl7_output/. Use the REST API at https://localhost:8443/api (admin/admin) with the 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI' header. The channel XML must include a <responseTransformer> element in destination connectors. The web dashboard at https://localhost:8443 is available for monitoring only.

You are the Integration Architect for a large regional health system with 8 hospitals. All external systems connect to a single HL7 'front door' endpoint, and messages must be dynamically routed to the correct downstream processing system based on both sending application (MSH-3) and message type (MSH-9). Build a three-channel enterprise routing solution consisting of an 'Enterprise HL7 Router', a 'Lab Results Processor', and an 'ADT Event Handler'.

The 'Enterprise HL7 Router' must listen on TCP port 6668 (MLLP). It must route messages to the 'Lab Results Processor' if the sending application (MSH-3) contains 'Lab' or the message type (MSH-9) starts with 'ORU'. It must route messages to the 'ADT Event Handler' if the message type starts with 'ADT'. All unmatched messages must be inserted into a 'dead_letter_queue' table.

The 'Lab Results Processor' must receive messages from the router and insert them into a 'lab_results_inbox' table. The 'ADT Event Handler' must receive messages from the router and insert them into an 'adt_events_inbox' table.

Create the following tables in the PostgreSQL database (jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb, credentials: postgres/postgres):
- dead_letter_queue: message_id VARCHAR, sending_app VARCHAR, message_type VARCHAR, raw_message TEXT, received_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
- lab_results_inbox: message_id VARCHAR, patient_mrn VARCHAR, test_code VARCHAR, received_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
- adt_events_inbox: message_id VARCHAR, patient_mrn VARCHAR, event_type VARCHAR, received_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()

A 'routing_rules' table is pre-seeded in the database for reference. All channels must be deployed and functional. Access the system via the REST API at https://localhost:8443/api (admin:admin) using the 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI' header.

You are a Healthcare IT Integration Specialist at a reference laboratory that receives nightly batch HL7 files from 12 hospital clients. To eliminate a manual processing backlog, build and deploy a NextGen Connect channel named 'Nightly HL7 Batch Processor'. The channel must poll the directory /home/ga/hl7_batch_inbox/ for files matching the pattern *.hl7 every 30 seconds. It should process these batch files by stripping BHS/BTS envelopes and splitting the content into individual HL7 messages. For each message, extract the patient MRN from PID-3 and the message type from MSH-9, then insert a record into the PostgreSQL table 'batch_processing_log' with the following columns: batch_file (VARCHAR(255)), message_seq (INTEGER), patient_mrn (VARCHAR(50)), message_type (VARCHAR(20)), and processed_at (TIMESTAMP). After processing, move the files to /home/ga/hl7_batch_archive/. Ensure the required directories and table are created during setup.

Environment Details:
- Sample batch file: /home/ga/hl7_batch_inbox/nightly_batch_20240115.hl7
- PostgreSQL: jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb (user: postgres, password: postgres)
- Direct DB access: docker exec nextgen-postgres psql -U postgres -d mirthdb
- REST API: https://localhost:8443/api (admin:admin)
- API Header: X-Requested-With: OpenAPI

You are a Health Informatics Specialist at City General Hospital. To prevent clinicians from missing life-threatening critical value alerts, implement a NextGen Connect channel named 'Lab Critical Value Router'. The channel must listen on TCP port 6664 for HL7 ORU^R01 messages using MLLP framing. It should utilize a JavaScript transformer to check the OBX-8 field of all OBX segments for critical indicators 'HH' or 'LL', setting a channel map variable 'isCritical' to 'true' if detected.

The channel requires three destinations:
- 'Critical DB Writer': A Database Writer that filters for 'isCritical' equals 'true' and inserts data into the PostgreSQL table 'critical_lab_results' (columns: message_id VARCHAR, patient_mrn VARCHAR, test_code VARCHAR, test_value VARCHAR, abnormal_flag VARCHAR, observation_time VARCHAR, received_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()).
- 'Normal DB Writer': A Database Writer that filters for 'isCritical' not equaling 'true' and inserts data into the PostgreSQL table 'normal_lab_results' (same column structure).
- 'Audit Log Writer': A File Writer with no filter that writes all messages to /tmp/lab_audit/ inside the container.

The channel must be deployed and in a STARTED state. Sample messages are provided at /home/ga/sample_critical_oru.hl7 and /home/ga/sample_normal_oru.hl7. The PostgreSQL database is accessible at jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb (username: postgres, password: postgres). The REST API is at https://localhost:8443/api (credentials: admin/admin) and requires the header 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI'. The web dashboard at https://localhost:8443 is for monitoring only.

You are a Health Information Management (HIM) Specialist maintaining the Patient Master Index (PMI) for a multi-facility hospital system. Build and deploy a NextGen Connect channel named 'Patient Master Index Sync' that listens on TCP port 6665 for HL7 ADT messages (A28, A31, A08). The channel must use a JavaScript transformer to extract mrn (PID-3, first repetition, CX.1 component), last_name (PID-5.1), first_name (PID-5.2), dob (PID-7, formatted as YYYYMMDD), gender (PID-8), and ssn (PID-19) into channel map variables. Implement a Database Writer destination to perform a PostgreSQL UPSERT into the patient_master_index table (where mrn is the primary key) using the logic: 'INSERT INTO patient_master_index (mrn, last_name, first_name, dob, gender, ssn, last_updated, update_count) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (mrn) DO UPDATE SET last_name=EXCLUDED.last_name, first_name=EXCLUDED.first_name, dob=EXCLUDED.dob, gender=EXCLUDED.gender, ssn=EXCLUDED.ssn, last_updated=NOW(), update_count=patient_master_index.update_count+1'. Include a Response Transformer that constructs a valid HL7 ACK message with acknowledgment code 'AA' using the MSH-10 Message Control ID from the incoming message. A sample ADT A31 message is located at /home/ga/sample_adt_a31.hl7. PostgreSQL is accessible at jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb (username: postgres, password: postgres) or via 'docker exec nextgen-postgres psql -U postgres -d mirthdb'. The REST API is available at https://localhost:8443/api (admin/admin) and requires the header 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI'. The channel XML must include a <responseTransformer> element.

Process the HL7 ADT^A01 message located at /home/ga/sample_adt_message.hl7 through the 'HL7 Message Processor' channel in NextGen Connect. The channel is listening on TCP port 6661 for MLLP-framed messages. Verify the message receipt in the channel statistics using the web dashboard or REST API at https://localhost:8443 (credentials: admin/admin, required header for API: X-Requested-With: OpenAPI).

Create a NextGen Connect channel named 'Patient DB Writer' that extracts patient data from the PID segment of HL7 messages and writes it to the 'patient_records' table in a PostgreSQL database. The channel must listen on TCP port 6663 and populate columns for patient_id, last_name, first_name, date of birth, and gender. The destination connector XML must include a <responseTransformer> element. Database connection: jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb (username: postgres, password: postgres). A sample ADT message is available at /home/ga/sample_adt_message.hl7. Use the REST API at https://localhost:8443/api (admin/admin, header 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI') for configuration; the web dashboard is monitoring-only.

You are a Clinical Systems Integration Engineer at a multi-site health system. Build a two-channel HL7 bridge in NextGen Connect to pre-register patients as ADT^A04 encounters based on SIU^S12 appointment booking messages.

The 'SIU Intake Channel' must listen on TCP port 6666 (MLLP) for SIU^S12 messages and transform them into ADT^A04 messages. This transformation requires mapping patient demographics (PID-3 MRN, PID-5 name, PID-7 DOB, PID-8 gender) and the appointment start datetime (SCH-11). The resulting ADT message must be stored in the channel map variable 'transformedADT' and forwarded to the 'ADT Pre-Registration Processor' channel.

The 'ADT Pre-Registration Processor' channel must receive the messages and use a Database Writer to insert the appointment ID, MRN, name, and appointment datetime into the 'scheduling_preregistrations' table in the PostgreSQL database (jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb, user: postgres, password: postgres). The target table 'scheduling_preregistrations' must include columns for appt_id (VARCHAR(50)), patient_mrn (VARCHAR(50)), patient_name (VARCHAR(200)), appt_datetime (VARCHAR(50)), and registered_at (TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()).

A sample SIU S12 message is located at /home/ga/sample_siu_s12.hl7. The NextGen Connect REST API is available at https://localhost:8443/api (admin/admin) and requires the header 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI'. Both channels must be fully configured and deployed.

Create a channel named 'HL7 Transformer Channel' in NextGen Connect to transform HL7 v2.x messages into XML format. The channel must use a TCP Listener source and a File Writer destination, with transformation logic to convert HL7 to XML and a <responseTransformer> element included in the destination connectors. Use the sample HL7 ORU^R01 message at /home/ga/sample_oru_message.hl7 for testing. Access the system via the terminal or the REST API at https://localhost:8443/api (credentials: admin/admin, header: 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI'). The web dashboard at https://localhost:8443 is monitoring-only. Note that File Writer output files are created within the Docker container filesystem.

You are a sustainability consultant hired by a beverage company to compare the environmental impact of two packaging alternatives: glass bottles and aluminum cans. Format the CSV so each row represents one packaging option, with columns for each impact category's calculated value.

You are an industrial ecologist conducting an uncertainty analysis for a carbon footprint study of electricity generation. The analysis must utilize a product system for a coal electricity generation process with at least three process input parameters defined by uncertainty distributions. Perform a Monte Carlo simulation with at least 500 iterations and export the mean GWP, standard deviation, and 95% confidence interval to ~/LCA_Results/monte_carlo_results.csv.

You are a sustainability specialist hired to identify environmental hotspots in the life cycle of a construction material. An IT manager at a health informatics company is modernizing the Oracle HR database archival strategy. Save a partition analysis report showing row counts per partition to /home/ga/Desktop/archive_analysis.txt. All objects must be valid and the archive must contain all original JOB_HISTORY rows.

The HR analytics team has accumulated 12,000 employee performance reviews in the PERF_REVIEWS table (owned by HR schema, credentials hr/hr123), all with STATUS='NEW'. A plant manager needs an OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) dashboard based on production data located at C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\PowerBITasks\production_log.csv. Create a Power BI report that calculates the following measures:

- Availability_Pct: (Sum of Scheduled_Time_Min - Sum of Downtime_Min) / Sum of Scheduled_Time_Min
- Quality_Pct: (Sum of Total_Units - Sum of Scrap_Units) / Sum of Total_Units
- Performance_Pct: (Sum of Total_Units * (Average of Ideal_Cycle_Seconds / 60)) / (Sum of Scheduled_Time_Min - Sum of Downtime_Min)
- OEE_Score: Availability_Pct * Quality_Pct * Performance_Pct

The dashboard must include a Gauge Chart showing the OEE_Score with a target of 0.85, and a Matrix visual displaying Machine_ID as rows with the four calculated measures as values. Create a conditions file for an Eriksen Flanker task experiment in PsychoPy. The Flanker task (Eriksen & Eriksen, 1974) measures selective attention: participants respond to a central arrow while ignoring surrounding flanking arrows that may be congruent or incongruent. The file must include at least 8 rows of trial data covering congruent trials (e.g., <<<<<, >>>>>) and incongruent trials (e.g., <<><<, >><>>) for both 'left' and 'right' directions. The 'corrAns' column should indicate the correct response key based on the central arrow direction, and the 'condition' column should be labeled 'congruent' or 'incongruent'.

You are an experimental psychologist at an anxiety research lab. A clinical neuropsychology lab needs to implement a P300 auditory oddball paradigm in PsychoPy Builder for EEG research. The paradigm uses a frequent 'standard' tone (1000 Hz, 80% of trials) and an infrequent 'deviant' tone (2000 Hz, 20% of trials). Participants press a button only when they detect the deviant.

- Conditions file: 300 rows total (240 standard, 60 deviant) with columns 'tone_hz', 'trigger_code' (1=standard, 2=deviant), and 'is_target' (0/1).
- Stimulus: 20ms tone duration.
- Timing: Jittered inter-stimulus interval (ISI) between 900-1100ms.
- Triggers: A code component must print the trigger code to the console at stimulus onset.
- Structure: At least 2 blocks of trials with a rest screen between them.
- Feedback: A final screen showing hit rate and false alarm rate.
