Export the resulting layer, ensuring the cluster ID field is included, as a GeoJSON file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/clustered_cities.geojson.

Create a new GeoPackage LineString layer named 'power_line' (CRS EPSG:4326) at /home/ga/GIS_Data/power_line.gpkg. Digitize a single route connecting Point A to Point B to Point C in that order, using snapping to ensure exact connectivity to the vertices. Save the layer edits and the project.

Dissolve the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' shapefile from /home/ga/GIS_Data/natural_earth/ into continent regions using the 'CONTINENT' field. Export the resulting dissolved layer as a GeoJSON file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/continents_dissolved.geojson.

Calculate the distance from major South American cities to the nearest coastline using the populated places layer (/home/ga/GIS_Data/natural_earth/ne_10m_populated_places_simple.shp) and the coastline layer (/home/ga/GIS_Data/natural_earth/ne_10m_coastline.shp). Filter the cities to include only those in South America and ensure calculations use the South America Albers Equal Area Conic projection (EPSG:102033). Export the results to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/sa_cities_coast_dist.csv as a CSV file containing the city 'name' and the distance to the coastline in kilometers in a field named 'dist_km'.

Create a 3D elevation path for a hiking trail by draping the 2D trail vector (/home/ga/GIS_Data/proposed_trail_2d.geojson) over the Digital Elevation Model raster (/home/ga/GIS_Data/srtm_41_19.tif). Ensure the resulting layer contains valid Z values from the raster and export the final 3D trail as a GeoPackage to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/trail_3d.gpkg.

Perform a suitability analysis to identify developable land by excluding restricted zones. Using the land parcels from /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson and radar stations from /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_points.geojson, identify areas that are at least 1 kilometer (1000 meters) away from the radar stations. Ensure analysis is performed using EPSG:32610 (WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N). Export the resulting developable areas to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/developable_areas.geojson in GeoJSON format.

A maritime logistics analyst needs to separate countries stored as MultiPolygon features into individual landmass polygons for independent analysis. Convert the MultiPolygon features from '/home/ga/GIS_Data/ne_110m_countries/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp' into singlepart Polygon features. Export the resulting GeoJSON FeatureCollection to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/countries_singlepart.geojson', ensuring all original attributes are preserved.

Generate index contours from the elevation raster located at /home/ga/GIS_Data/raster/srtm_41_19.tif. Use a base contour interval of 20 meters with an elevation attribute named 'ELEV'. Extract only the index contours (elevations that are multiples of 100 meters) and export the result as a GeoJSON file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/index_contours.geojson.

Identify scenic roads by performing a spatial selection. Using the global roads dataset (/home/ga/GIS_Data/natural_earth/ne_10m_roads.shp) and the parks dataset (/home/ga/GIS_Data/natural_earth/ne_10m_parks_and_protected_lands.shp), extract all road segments that intersect with parks or protected lands. Save the resulting features as a GeoJSON file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/scenic_roads.geojson.

A land surveyor needs a table of all corner coordinates from planning zone polygon boundaries for GPS field verification. Using the polygon layer from /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson, extract the corner vertices into a point layer and add explicit X (longitude) and Y (latitude) coordinate columns. Export the resulting point layer as a CSV file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/polygon_vertices.csv, ensuring it contains all attribute columns including coordinates, vertex_index, and original polygon attributes like name and id. Save the QGIS project to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/vertex_extraction.qgs.

Load the world countries dataset from /home/ga/GIS_Data/natural_earth/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp and calculate the GDP per capita for each country. Create a new field named 'gdp_per_capita' (Decimal number, length 20, precision 2) using the formula: ( "GDP_MD_EST" * 1000000 ) / "POP_EST". Export the final attribute table as a CSV file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/countries_gdp_per_capita.csv.

A colleague sent you a Shapefile of San Francisco landmarks ('sf_landmarks_missing_prj.shp') missing its projection file. Using the coordinate system information found in 'surveyor_notes.txt', export the corrected layer as a WGS84 GeoJSON file to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/sf_landmarks_wgs84.geojson'.

Perform a flood risk assessment for Liechtenstein using OpenStreetMap data. Using the waterways (gis_osm_waterways_free_1.shp) and populated places (gis_osm_places_free_1.shp) layers located in /home/ga/GIS_Data/liechtenstein/, identify all populated places within a 1 kilometer dissolved buffer of the 'Rhein' (or 'Rhine') river. Export the identified features to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/at_risk_towns.geojson using EPSG:4326.

Convert the road network from /home/ga/GIS_Data/urban_roads.geojson into city block polygons. Calculate the area of each block in hectares and store the values in a new decimal field named 'area_ha'. Export the resulting polygon layer to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/city_blocks.geojson.

Georeference the scanned map image located at /home/ga/GIS_Data/historical_map.png using the pre-existing Ground Control Points (GCPs) from /home/ga/GIS_Data/historical_map.png.points. Use a Polynomial 1 (Affine) or Helmert transformation type with Nearest Neighbour resampling and set the target CRS to EPSG:4326 (WGS 84). Export the georeferenced raster to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/historical_map_georeferenced.tif.

Create a thematic choropleth map showing world population using the vector layer at /home/ga/GIS_Data/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson. Visualize the data using the 'POP_EST' field classified into at least 5 classes. Save the project to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/world_population_map.qgz and export the map as a PNG image (minimum 800x600 pixels) to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/world_population_map.png.

Generate a population density heatmap using the global city data from /home/ga/GIS_Data/natural_earth/ne_110m_populated_places_simple.shp. Use the Kernel Density Estimation method with a radius of 5.0 map units, weighting the density by the pop_max field, and setting the pixel size to 0.5. Save the resulting raster to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/population_heatmap.tif and save the QGIS project as /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/heatmap_project.qgz.

Perform a hexagonal binning (hexbin) analysis to visualize urban population density in Europe using the populated places layer located at '/home/ga/GIS_Data/ne_10m_populated_places/ne_10m_populated_places.shp'. Generate a hexagonal grid covering the European extent (West: -15, South: 35, East: 45, North: 72) with horizontal and vertical spacing of 3 degrees. For each hexagon, calculate the number of cities and the sum of their population using the 'POP_MAX' field. Filter the analysis to include only hexagons that contain at least one city (count > 0) and export the final result as a GeoJSON to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/europe_hexbin_population.geojson'.

Identify land parcels from '/home/ga/GIS_Data/cadastral/subdivision_parcels.geojson' that are isolated from the road network provided in '/home/ga/GIS_Data/cadastral/road_network.geojson'. Create a 10-meter easement buffer around only those parcels that do not share a boundary with any road. Ensure accurate metric buffering by using the 'WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N' (EPSG:32610) projection. Save the final buffered result to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/easement_buffers.geojson'.

Perform an Inverse Distance Weighting (IDW) interpolation on earthquake magnitude data from /home/ga/GIS_Data/california_earthquakes.geojson using the 'mag' attribute to create a continuous raster surface. Ensure the output raster has a pixel size of approximately 0.05 degrees (or at least 100x100 pixels). Export the resulting GeoTIFF to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/earthquake_magnitude_surface.tif and save the QGIS project to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/earthquake_interpolation.qgz.

You are a conservation GIS analyst at the US Fish & Wildlife Service tracking the spread of the invasive Asian lady beetle (Harmonia axyridis) across the contiguous United States. Management budgets must be allocated to states where the invasion is actively expanding.

Using occurrence records from GBIF and US state boundaries, compare two time periods to classify each state's invasion status and export a management priority report.

Input files:
- /home/ga/GIS_Data/harmonia_us_occurrences.geojson (GBIF occurrence records, 2005-2023, with 'year' property for each point)
- /home/ga/GIS_Data/us_states.geojson (Contiguous US state boundaries with 'name' and 'postal' fields)

Deliverable 1: Export a GeoJSON FeatureCollection of contiguous US states to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/invasion_status_by_state.geojson. Include only states with at least 1 occurrence in either time period. Each feature must retain original state fields plus:
- count_2005_2012 (integer): occurrences within state boundary during 2005-2012
- count_2016_2023 (integer): occurrences within state boundary during 2016-2023
- pct_change (float, 1 decimal): 100*(count_2016_2023 - count_2005_2012)/count_2005_2012; use null/None if count_2005_2012 is 0
- invasion_status (string): 'expanding' if count_2016_2023 > count_2005_2012 and count_2005_2012 > 0; 'new_invasion' if count_2005_2012 == 0 and count_2016_2023 > 0; 'established' if count_2016_2023 <= count_2005_2012 and count_2005_2012 > 0; 'no_recent_activity' if count_2016_2023 == 0 and count_2005_2012 > 0

Deliverable 2: Export a summary report to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/invasion_summary.csv with columns: invasion_status, state_count, total_occurrences_recent.

Enrich the country boundary layer /home/ga/GIS_Data/ne_countries_geometry.geojson with economic and demographic data from /home/ga/GIS_Data/country_statistics.csv. Perform an attribute join using the ISO_A3 field as the common key. Ensure the resulting layer includes the POP_EST and GDP_MD fields. Export the joined layer to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/countries_with_statistics.geojson.

Calculate land cover statistics for management zones using the vector layer at /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson and the land cover raster at /home/ga/GIS_Data/rasters/landcover.tif (where 1=Water, 2=Forest, 3=Urban). Calculate the percentage of Water pixels (Class 1) relative to the total sum of all classes (1+2+3) and store it in a new decimal field named 'pct_water'. Export the final result, including the histogram counts and the new percentage field, to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/zone_composition.geojson.

Perform a land use change analysis by executing a Union overlay with /home/ga/GIS_Data/landuse_2015.geojson as the input layer and /home/ga/GIS_Data/landuse_2025.geojson as the overlay layer. In the resulting layer, calculate the area of each feature in square kilometers (using $area / 1000000) and store it in a new decimal field named transition_area_sqkm. Export the attribute table containing the land use classes from both years and the calculated area to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/change_matrix.csv.

Perform a vector overlay intersection to split road lines from /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_lines.geojson at the polygon zone boundaries defined in /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson. Ensure the resulting features contain attributes from both input layers and export the final result to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/roads_by_zone.geojson.

Load the GeoJSON layers from /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson and /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_points.geojson into a new QGIS project. Style the polygon layer with a semi-transparent blue fill (50-70% opacity). Style the point layer with red markers at a size of 4.0 mm. Save the project to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/styled_layers.qgz.

Create a country-level dataset summarizing megacities (population > 5 million) using the country boundaries from /home/ga/GIS_Data/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson and populated places from /home/ga/GIS_Data/ne_110m_populated_places.geojson. For each country, calculate the count of megacities and the total population of those megacities (sum of POP_MAX). Export the resulting dataset as a GeoJSON file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/megacity_stats_by_country.geojson.

Three regional country boundary files have been prepared in `/home/ga/GIS_Data/regions/`: `europe_countries.geojson`, `africa_countries.geojson`, and `south_america_countries.geojson`. Your task is to merge these three separate datasets into a single unified vector layer using QGIS. Save the merged result as a GeoJSON file to `/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/merged_countries.geojson`. Ensure the output contains all countries from the three input regions, preserves their original attributes (NAME, CONTINENT, etc.), and maintains the Coordinate Reference System (CRS) as WGS 84 (EPSG:4326).

Merge the two elevation raster tiles dem_tile_west.tif and dem_tile_east.tif from /home/ga/GIS_Data/rasters/ into a single seamless Digital Elevation Model (DEM). Save the merged output as a Float32 file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/merged_dem.tif, ensuring it covers the combined extent of both input tiles.

An emergency management analyst needs to create concentric evacuation zones around three hazard locations in the San Francisco Bay Area. Using the point data from /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_points.geojson, generate a multi-ring buffer with 3 rings at 500-meter intervals. Ensure the processing uses a metric CRS (EPSG:32610) for accurate distance measurement. Export the final evacuation zones as a GeoJSON file in WGS84 (EPSG:4326) to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/evacuation_zones.geojson.

Compute a nearest-neighbor distance matrix for western US state capitals using /home/ga/GIS_Data/us_western_capitals.geojson. The output must be a linear (N*k x 3) matrix showing the distance to the nearest other capital for each entry. Export the results to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/capital_distance_matrix.csv and save the project to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/distance_analysis.qgz.

Calculate the shortest delivery route from the Warehouse to the Customer using the road network provided in /home/ga/GIS_Data/road_network.geojson. Use the Warehouse (South-West corner) and Customer (North-East corner) points from /home/ga/GIS_Data/logistics_stops.geojson as the start and end points. Export the resulting shortest path layer as a GeoJSON file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/delivery_route.geojson.

A field survey team needs all project GIS data consolidated into a single GeoPackage (.gpkg) file for their mobile mapping application. Consolidate the polygon zones (/home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson), monitoring stations (/home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_points.geojson), and road network (/home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_lines.geojson) into a single GeoPackage file at /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/project_data.gpkg. The layers within the GeoPackage must be named 'zones', 'stations', and 'roads' respectively. Save the QGIS project to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/delivery_project.qgs.

A utility company needs regularly-spaced inspection waypoints along their line infrastructure. Load the line layer from /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_lines.geojson (EPSG:4326) and generate points along the geometry at a distance of 0.05 degrees with no start or end offsets. Export the resulting points layer in GeoJSON format to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/inspection_points.geojson, ensuring the CRS remains EPSG:4326.

Calculate the Pole of Inaccessibility for Somalia using the Natural Earth countries dataset located at /home/ga/GIS_Data/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson. Ensure calculations are performed using EPSG:3857 with a tolerance of 1000 meters. Export the resulting point feature to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/somalia_pole.geojson and record the distance to the boundary in kilometers in a text file at /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/distance_report.txt.

Create a professional print layout map in QGIS using the administrative zones polygon layer (/home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson) and monitoring stations point layer (/home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_points.geojson). Style the layers to be visually distinguishable and create a print layout named 'Bay Area Report' that includes a map item, a legend, a scale bar, and a title label 'Bay Area Monitoring Stations'. Export the final layout as a PNG image to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/bay_area_map.png and save the QGIS project to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/bay_area_map.qgz.

A conservation biologist is planning a biodiversity survey across three Central European countries. Using the boundaries for Austria, Switzerland, and Czechia provided in /home/ga/GIS_Data/european_countries.geojson, generate 5 random sampling points within each country. Ensure the resulting points layer includes 'longitude' and 'latitude' fields for each point. Export the sampling points as a GeoJSON to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/sampling_points.geojson and the attribute table as a CSV to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/sampling_coordinates.csv.

Convert the land cover raster at /home/ga/GIS_Data/landcover_classification.tif (1=Forest, 2=Water, 3=Urban) to vector polygons. Filter the resulting layer to include only the Forest features (DN=1) and export them to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/forest_zones.geojson.

Convert the polygon vector layer at /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson into a raster GeoTIFF using the Rasterize (Vector to Raster) tool. Use the 'area_sqkm' field for the burn-in value and set the output resolution to 0.005 georeferenced units (degrees) for both horizontal and vertical. Ensure the output extent matches the input layer extent and set the nodata value to -9999. Save the GeoTIFF to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/polygon_raster.tif and the QGIS project to /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/rasterize_project.qgs.

Clean and standardize the 'legacy_stations' layer by refactoring its fields. Rename 'STN_ID_X' to 'station_id' (String), 'LOC_NM' to 'name' (String), 'READ_VAL' to 'temperature' (changing the type to Double/Real), and 'OBS_DT' to 'date' (Date). Remove the 'LEGACY_CD' and 'TECH_N' fields. Export the processed layer to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/clean_stations.geojson.

Clean the land parcel dataset at /home/ga/GIS_Data/parcels_with_errors.geojson by repairing invalid geometries, such as self-intersections. Export the repaired layer to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/parcels_fixed.geojson in GeoJSON format.

Reproject the road network layer from /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_lines.geojson (EPSG:4326) to EPSG:32610 and save it to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/roads_utm.geojson. Calculate the length of each road in kilometers in a new decimal field named 'length_km'. Export the resulting data to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/road_measurements.csv.

Generate perpendicular transects along the Danube River for a hydrological survey using the dataset located at /home/ga/GIS_Data/natural_earth/ne_10m_rivers_lake_centerlines.shp. Create transects with a spacing of 20,000 meters and a length of 10,000 meters. Export the resulting transects as a GeoJSON file to /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/danube_transects.geojson.

You are an Information Security Analyst at a regional healthcare organization. Your SIEM system has detected a critical security incident: a patient records API endpoint was deployed without authentication middleware, potentially exposing Protected Health Information (PHI) for 847 patients to an external IP address over a 6-hour window. This triggers HIPAA Breach Notification Rule requirements. Log in to Rocket.Chat (Username: admin, Password: Admin1234!) to manage the incident response.

Star the unauthorized access alert in the #security-monitoring channel and reply to the alert in a thread confirming that the HIPAA incident response process has been initiated. Create a private channel named 'hipaa-inc-2026-0306' with the topic 'HIPAA Breach Investigation | PHI Exposure via /api/v2/patients/records | 847 patients affected' and invite privacy.officer, legal.counsel, it.director, and sys.admin. Post and pin a structured incident report in this channel containing sections for: (a) Incident Summary, (b) Affected Data (patient names, DOBs, MRNs, ICD-10 codes), (c) Timeline (discovery to containment), (d) Containment Actions Taken, and (e) Regulatory Requirements (HIPAA 45 CFR 164.400-414 breach notification).

Inform privacy.officer via direct message that the 60-day HIPAA breach notification clock has started and request they prepare individual notification letters for the 847 affected patients. Request legal.counsel via direct message to initiate a legal hold on all related system logs and advise on HHS Office for Civil Rights notification requirements. Document the incident in the #compliance-log channel, including the date, incident type (PHI exposure), number of affected patients, and the remediation channel reference.

You are an atmospheric scientist at a climate research institute analyzing the NASA/GISS Surface Temperature Analysis (GISTEMP v4) global mean surface temperature anomaly record. 1. Decompose the annual temperature anomaly time series (1880–2023) using STL decomposition (stats::stl()). Write the decomposition components to /home/ga/RProjects/output/climate_stl_components.csv with columns: year, observed, trend, seasonal, remainder. Use annual averages computed from the monthly data (columns Jan–Dec, skipping non-numeric entries like '***').

2. Fit an ARIMA model (auto.arima from the forecast package) to the trend component extracted from STL. Forecast 10 years ahead (2024–2033). Write forecasts to /home/ga/RProjects/output/climate_forecast.csv with columns: year, forecast, lower80, upper80, lower95, upper95.

3. Detect structural breakpoints in the annual trend component using the changepoint package (cpt.mean() or cpt.var()). Write breakpoint results to /home/ga/RProjects/output/climate_breakpoints.csv with columns: breakpoint_year, segment_mean_before, segment_mean_after.

4. Generate a 3-panel time series figure showing: (top) observed annual anomaly with trend overlay, (middle) ARIMA forecast with 80%/95% confidence bands extending to 2033, (bottom) changepoint detection visualization with segment means. Save to /home/ga/RProjects/output/climate_analysis.png.

Save open curve markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/aortic_curve.mrk.json, straight-line ruler to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/aortic_straight.mrk.json, and report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/evar_measurements.json with fields: path_length_mm, straight_length_mm, tortuosity_ratio, superior_landmark, inferior_landmark.

Measure the abdominal aorta diameter at three levels (suprarenal, infrarenal, and pre-bifurcation) on the provided CT, calculate tapering ratios, and identify any focal dilations. Save measurements markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/aortic_measurements.mrk.json and report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/tapering_report.json with fields: suprarenal_diameter_mm, suprarenal_vertebral_level, infrarenal_diameter_mm, infrarenal_vertebral_level, bifurcation_diameter_mm, bifurcation_vertebral_level, infrarenal_suprarenal_ratio, bifurcation_suprarenal_ratio, focal_dilation_present, focal_dilation_max_diameter_mm, focal_dilation_location, tapering_assessment, clinical_recommendation.

Assess aortic tortuosity on the provided abdominal CT. Document the aortic centerline from the diaphragmatic hiatus to the bifurcation using fiducial points. Save the centerline fiducials to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/aorta_centerline.mrk.json. Report the chord_length_mm, arc_length_mm, tortuosity_index_percent, classification, and num_centerline_points to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/tortuosity_report.json.

Change the display colormap of the loaded MRHead brain MRI volume from the default grayscale to a color-based lookup table (e.g., Ocean, Hot, or Rainbow). The colormap must not be a grayscale variant. No file output is required.

Apply Gaussian smoothing to the loaded MRHead brain MRI volume with a sigma of approximately 2.0 mm. Produce a new volume named 'MRHead_smoothed' and ensure the original MRHead volume remains unchanged.

Create a LinearTransform with a rotation of approximately 15 degrees around the IS (Inferior-Superior) axis and apply it to the loaded MRHead brain MRI volume. Keep the transform as an association (not hardened). No file output is required.

Measure white matter and gray matter intensity values on the loaded T1-weighted brain MRI. Calculate the WM/GM ratio and assess image quality. Save the results to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/wm_gm_contrast.json with the fields: wm_intensity, gm_intensity, wm_gm_ratio, wm_position_ras, gm_position_ras, quality_assessment, and notes.

Measure the short-axis diameter of the azygos vein at the azygos arch on the provided chest CT and classify the finding. Save measurement markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/azygos_measurement.mrk.json and report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/azygos_report.json with fields: diameter_mm, classification, slice_level, interpretation.

Measure the bicaudate index (BCI) on the provided T1-weighted brain MRI. Save the intercaudate distance measurement to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/intercaudate_measurement.mrk.json and the brain width measurement at the level of the frontal horns to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/brain_width_measurement.mrk.json. Record the results in a report at ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/bicaudate_report.json including the fields: intercaudate_distance_mm, brain_width_mm, bicaudate_index, classification, and slice_number.

Segment the urinary bladder on the provided abdominal CT and measure its volume. Save segmentation to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/bladder_segmentation.nii.gz and report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/bladder_report.json with fields: volume_ml, distension_status, clinical_significance.

Perform opportunistic bone density screening on the provided chest CT. Measure the mean HU within an ROI placed in the trabecular bone of a lower thoracic or upper lumbar vertebral body and classify the bone density status. Save the ROI to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/bone_density_roi.seg.nrrd and report the findings to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/bone_density_report.json with the following fields: vertebral_level, mean_hu, roi_area_mm2, classification, and clinical_notes.

Localize the glioma on the provided brain FLAIR MRI. Mark the tumor centroid with a fiducial, capture axial, coronal, and sagittal screenshots, and document the laterality. Save the outputs to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/LocalizationReport/ as lesion_axial.png, lesion_coronal.png, lesion_sagittal.png, lesion_centroid.mrk.json, and localization_report.json with fields for lesion_centroid_ras, laterality, midline_distance_mm, and hemisphere_involvement.

Measure the maximum brain midline shift on the provided brain MRI. Document the shift distance (mm) and direction (left/right). Save measurement markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/midline_measurement.mrk.json and report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/midline_shift_report.json with fields: shift_mm, direction, severity, slice_location, clinical_recommendation.

Create a complete brain tumor documentation package from the loaded glioma MRI (FLAIR and T1_Contrast). Perform bidimensional measurements of the tumor and capture annotated screenshots in axial, sagittal, and coronal planes. Save screenshots to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/Documentation/axial_view.png, ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/Documentation/sagittal_view.png, and ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/Documentation/coronal_view.png. Save measurement markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/Documentation/measurements.mrk.json. Save the report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/Documentation/documentation_report.json with fields: patient_id, finding, max_axial_diameter_mm, perpendicular

Segment the complete tumor region on the loaded brain MRI (FLAIR, T1, T1ce, T2 sequences) and create a 3D visualization. Save the segmentation to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/agent_segmentation.nii.gz and report the tumor volume in mL to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/tumor_report.txt.

Measure bronchial wall thickness on the provided chest CT to assess airway remodeling. Measure the outer and inner diameters of a segmental bronchus and calculate Wall Area Percentage (WA%). Save measurement markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/bronchial_measurements.mrk.json and the report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/bronchial_report.json with fields: outer_diameter_mm, inner_diameter_mm, wall_area_percentage, classification, bronchus_location, slice_number.

Calculate the Evans Index on the loaded FLAIR brain MRI by measuring the maximum frontal horn width and maximum internal skull diameter at the same axial level (within 5mm Z-coordinate tolerance). Export the line markups to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/evans_measurements.mrk.json.

Measure the liver-to-spleen (L/S) attenuation ratio on the provided abdominal CT (AMOS dataset) for hepatic steatosis assessment. Save the liver and spleen ROI statistics (mean and standard deviation), the calculated L/S ratio, your interpretation, and the slice number used to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/ls_ratio_result.json using the following fields: liver_roi_hu_mean, liver_roi_hu_std, spleen_roi_hu_mean, spleen_roi_hu_std, ls_ratio, interpretation, slice_number.

Calculate quantitative statistics for the pre-loaded brain tumor segmentation (Necrotic, Edema, Enhancing segments) against the T1ce MRI volume. Export results as a CSV to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/tumor_statistics.csv containing all three segments with volume measurements and at least one intensity statistic.

Calculate the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) for the loaded MRHead brain MRI. The SNR is defined as the mean of a 'Signal' segment in the brain white matter divided by the standard deviation of a 'Noise' segment in the background air, where each segment must contain at least 100 voxels. Save the results, including signal_mean, noise_std, and snr, to /home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/snr_result.json.
Perform a 3D volume rendering of the MRHead.nrrd volume (located at ~/Documents/SlicerData/SampleData/MRHead.nrrd) and save a screenshot of the rendered brain to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/.

Measure the RV and LV maximum transverse diameters on the chest CT at the 4-chamber level and calculate the RV/LV ratio. Save the RV measurement to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Cardiac/rv_measurement.mrk.json, the LV measurement to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Cardiac/lv_measurement.mrk.json, and the report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Cardiac/cardiac_report.json with the fields: rv_diameter_mm, lv_diameter_mm, rv_lv_ratio, and classification.

Measure the cardiothoracic ratio (CTR) on the provided chest CT using the maximum transverse cardiac diameter and maximum internal thoracic diameter at the same axial level. Save the cardiac measurement to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/cardiac_diameter.mrk.json, the thoracic measurement to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/thoracic_diameter.mrk.json, and the report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/ctr_report.json with the following fields: cardiac_diameter_mm, thoracic_diameter_mm, ctr_ratio, slice_number, and classification.

Measure the carinal angle at the tracheal bifurcation on the provided chest CT using an angle markup with the vertex at the carina and arms along the right and left main bronchi. Save the markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Airway/carinal_angle.mrk.json and report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Airway/carinal_report.json with the fields: measured_angle_degrees, classification, and vertebral_level.

Measure the caudate-to-right-lobe (C/RL) ratio on the provided abdominal CT at the portal vein bifurcation level for cirrhosis assessment. Save the caudate measurement to ~/Documents/SlicerData/IRCADb/caudate_measurement.mrk.json, the right lobe measurement to ~/Documents/SlicerData/IRCADb/rightlobe_measurement.mrk.json, and the report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/IRCADb/crl_ratio_report.json. The report must include fields for caudate_width_mm, rightlobe_width_mm, crl_ratio, slice_level, classification, and supporting_findings.

Measure the common bile duct (CBD) internal diameter on the provided abdominal CT at the porta hepatis region. Save measurement markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/cbd_measurement.mrk.json and report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/cbd_report.json with fields: diameter_mm, anatomical_level, classification, interpretation.

Measure the cerebellar vermis anteroposterior (AP) diameter on the provided brain MRI (FLAIR sequence) using the mid-sagittal plane. Save measurement markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/vermis_measurement.mrk.json and report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/vermis_report.json with fields: ap_diameter (mm), morphology (string), classification (string).

Clean the pre-loaded lung segmentation ('Lungs' segment) by removing small disconnected artifact islands, keeping only the main lung structures. Save the cleaned segmentation to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/lungs_cleaned.seg.nrrd.

Create a clipped 3D volume rendering of the BraTS T1ce brain MRI (~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/) to reveal internal tumor structures. Use ROI cropping to remove 30-50% of the brain surface and save a screenshot of the cutaway rendering to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/clipped_brain_rendering.png.

Visualize the T1, T1ce, T2, and FLAIR MRI sequences from ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/ in a multi-panel layout where each viewer displays a unique sequence. Save a screenshot of the tumor region to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/mri_comparison.png.

Compare the AI-generated segmentation (~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/ai_segmentation.nii.gz) against the reference segmentation (/var/lib/slicer/ground_truth/BraTS2021_00000_seg.nii.gz) by computing Dice coefficient and Hausdorff distance. Configure the axial slice view on the loaded abdominal CT to display a 10mm thick-slab Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP) for enhanced vessel visualization. The slab mode should be set to 'Max' with a thickness of 10mm.

Configure the slice view annotations for the MRHead brain MRI (~/Documents/SlicerData/SampleData/MRHead.nrrd). Enable the orientation marker labels (A/P, R/L, S/I) and hide the patient name and study date displays across the Red, Yellow, and Green slice views.

Create a blended overlay of the pre-loaded FLAIR (background) and T1ce (foreground) brain MRI sequences with 50% foreground opacity. Save a screenshot of the result to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/overlay_result.png.

Measure the corpus callosum on the MRHead brain MRI at the mid-sagittal plane: total genu-to-splenium length and thickness at the genu, body, and splenium. Save measurement markups to ~/Documents/SlicerData/SampleData/cc_measurements.mrk.json and a report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/SampleData/corpus_callosum_report.json with fields: total_length_mm, genu_thickness_mm, body_thickness_mm, splenium_thickness_mm, mid_sagittal_slice, atrophy_grade.

Render the pre-loaded 'TumorSegmentation' on the BraTS brain MRI in 3D. Save a screenshot of the 3D visualization to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/tumor_3d_visualization.png.

Identify the anterior commissure (AC) and posterior commissure (PC) on the MRHead brain MRI (~/Documents/SlicerData/SampleData/MRHead.nrrd) at the midsagittal plane. Create an arrow markup labeled 'Left Lateral Ventricle' pointing to the left lateral ventricle on the pre-loaded MRHead brain MRI at an axial slice where the ventricles are visible. Save a screenshot of the annotated view to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/ventricle_annotation.png.

Create a curved planar reformation (CPR) of the abdominal aorta on the pre-loaded contrast-enhanced CT using an open curve of at least 8 control points spanning at least 100mm along the centerline. Set up a Four-Up layout displaying each of the four pre-loaded BraTS MRI sequences (T1, T1ce, T2, FLAIR) in a separate panel, linked at the same axial slice level with the tumor visible. Mirror the pre-loaded MRHead brain MRI along the LR (X) axis. Save the mirrored volume to /home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/MRHead_mirrored.nrrd and configure the workspace to show a comparison view of both the original and mirrored volumes.
Hollow the pre-loaded 'Liver' segment (IRCADb dataset) using a 3.0 mm shell thickness and inside surface mode. Ensure the segment remains named 'Liver'. No files need to be saved.
Generate a lightbox montage of the pre-loaded MRHead brain MRI with 5 columns and 4 rows (20 axial slices). Save the montage to /home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/brain_montage.png.

Create a skull-stripped brain volume from ~/Documents/SlicerData/SampleData/MRHead.nrrd. The resulting volume should have all non-brain regions zeroed out. Save the output to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/MRHead_brain_only.nrrd.

Create a coronal Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP) visualization of the pre-loaded chest CT (CTChest.nrrd) using slab mode with at least 30mm thickness. Save a screenshot of the coronal MIP view to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/chest_mip_coronal.png.

Create a subtraction enhancement map (T1ce minus T1) named 'EnhancementMap' using the BraTS brain MRI data at ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/ (sample ID in /tmp/brats_sample_id). Export the result to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/enhancement_map.nii.gz.

Crop the pre-loaded MRHead brain MRI to tightly enclose the brain region, removing empty background while preserving all brain tissue. Save the cropped volume to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/MRHead_cropped.nrrd.

Reduce the polygon count of the pre-loaded brain surface model by at least 50% while preserving recognizable anatomical shape. Save the model to /home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/brain_decimated.vtk.

Define the AC-PC reference plane on the MRHead brain MRI using a plane markup with three control points: the anterior commissure (AC), the posterior commissure (PC), and a superior midline point. Save the plane markup to ~/Documents/SlicerData/acpc_plane.mrk.json.

Save the screenshot to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/metadata_verification_screenshot.png.

Measure the L4-L5 intervertebral disc height (anterior and posterior) and a reference vertebral body height on the provided lumbar CT. Measure the maximum transverse diameter of the brain tumor on the loaded FLAIR MRI axial view. Create three concentric ring-shaped margin segments around the 'Tumor' segment on the BraTS brain MRI: 'Zone1_5mm' (red, 0-5mm), 'Zone2_10mm' (yellow, 5-10mm), and 'Zone3_15mm' (green, 10-15mm). Segment both lungs from the provided chest CT and quantify emphysema using the LAA-950 method. Calculate total lung volume, mean lung density, and 15th percentile density. Generate a subtraction enhancement map named 'EnhancementMap' (T1_Contrast minus T1) and a binary 'EnhancementMask' from the loaded brain MRI volumes. Measure the esophageal wall thickness on the provided chest CT and classify the finding. Estimate the tumor volume on the provided brain MRI (T1-contrast) using the ABC/2 ellipsoid method. Save markups to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/measurement_A.mrk.json, measurement_B.mrk.json, and measurement_C.mrk.json. Save report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/volume_estimate.txt with fields: A_diameter_mm, B_diameter_mm, C_diameter_mm, estimated_volume_ml.

Create a 5.0mm surgical margin named 'SurgicalMargin' by expanding the 'EnhancingTumor' segment in 'TumorSegmentation'. Save the segmentation to ~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/surgical_margin_segmentation.seg.nrrd.

Export the loaded MRHead brain MRI volume as a DICOM series with Patient Name set to 'SlicerExportTest' and Study Description set to 'Brain MRI Export Task'. Save the DICOM files to ~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/DICOM_Export/.

Measure the gallbladder on the provided abdominal CT, including longitudinal length, maximum transverse diameter, and wall thickness. Identify and mark the three major hepatic veins (RHV, MHV, LHV) at their IVC ostia on the contrast-enhanced abdominal CT. Measure mean Hounsfield Unit (HU) values for five tissue types (subcutaneous fat, skeletal muscle, liver parenchyma, aortic blood pool, and vertebral bone) on the loaded abdominal CT using labeled ROIs. Export the ROI markups to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/tissue_rois.mrk.json and save a report to ~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/hu_tissue_report.json with the fields: case_id, measurements (array of {tissue, mean_hu, classification, slice_z_mm}).

Estimate the intracerebral hemorrhage volume on the loaded head CT using the ABC/2 method. Add the missing abstracts to the following three papers in your Zotero library:

'Attention Is All You Need' (Vaswani et al., 2017)
Abstract: "The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks in an encoder-decoder configuration. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train."

'Generative Adversarial Nets' (Goodfellow et al., 2014)
Abstract: "We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models: a generative model G that captures the data distribution, and a discriminative model D that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data rather than G. The training procedure for G is to maximize the probability of D making a mistake. This framework corresponds to a minimax two-player game."

'Deep Learning' (LeCun, Bengio, Hinton, 2015)
Abstract: "Deep learning allows computational models that are composed of multiple processing layers to learn representations of data with multiple levels of abstraction. These methods have dramatically improved the state-of-the-art in speech recognition, visual object recognition, object detection and many other domains such as drug discovery and genomics. Deep learning discovers intricate structure in large data sets by using the backpropagation algorithm to indicate how a machine should change its internal parameters that are used to compute the representation in each layer from the representation in the previous layer."
